dwelling_habits.dwelling: Warm shallow seas — coral reefs, seagrass beds, and calm lagoons. Nests on sandy beaches; hatchlings scramble to the water at night.
dwelling_habits.food: Adults prefer seagrass and algae (they are mostly vegetarian). Young turtles snack more widely, including jellies and small invertebrates.
dwelling_habits.feeding: Grazes like a lawnmower on the seafloor, often resting between bites on sandy patches snorkelers can spot.
dwelling_habits.mating: Mates in the water near nesting beaches. Females return to lay eggs in sandy pits — hundreds of eggs, many trips per season.
dwelling_habits.socializing: Usually solitary travelers, but groups gather on good grazing flats. They recognize some feeding spots across years.

fun_facts: They are not named for a green shell — the fat under the shell is greenish from all that salad.
A turtle can hold its breath for hours while napping underwater… which makes “hold still for the photo” their specialty.
